The setup provides multiple preset configurations ranging from minimal logging to full trace-level output.\n\n## Configuration Script\n\nThe main configuration script is located at:\n```\n/home/coding/ARMOR/pluck-debug-config.sh\n```\n\nThis script provides preset configurations for different debug levels and handles log capture automatically.\n\n## Available Debug Presets\n\n### 1. **minimal** - INFO level\n- **RUST_LOG:** `needle::strand::pluck=info`\n- **Output:** High-level strand operations only\n- **Use case:** Quick health checks, basic operation verification\n\n### 2. **standard** - DEBUG level (Recommended)\n- **RUST_LOG:** `needle::strand::pluck=debug`\n- **Output:** Filtering decisions and statistics\n- **Use case:** Normal debugging, understanding filtering behavior\n\n### 3. **detailed** - TRACE level\n- **RUST_LOG:** `needle::strand::pluck=trace`\n- **Output:** Complete execution details\n- **Use case:** Deep troubleshooting, understanding exact flow\n\n### 4. **comprehensive** - Multi-module TRACE\n- **RUST_LOG:** `needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug`\n- **Output:** Pluck TRACE + supporting modules DEBUG\n- **Use case:** Full context debugging, understanding system interactions\n\n### 5. **full** - All NEEDLE modules\n- **RUST_LOG:** `needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug,needle::claim=debug`\n- **Output:** All critical modules at DEBUG/TRACE level\n- **Use case:** Complete system debugging\n\n### 6. **maximum** - Global TRACE\n- **RUST_LOG:** `trace`\n- **Output:** Everything at TRACE level (very verbose)\n- **Use case:** Deep system-level debugging\n\n## Usage\n\n### Basic Usage\n\n```bash\n# Run with standard debug level (recommended)\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log standard\n\n# Run with comprehensive debug level\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log comprehensive\n\n# Run with detailed trace level\n./crawl-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log detailed\n```\n\n### Advanced Usage\n\n```bash\n# Specify workspace, output file, mode, and run count\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR custom-output.log full 3\n\n# Show help\n./pluck-debug-config.sh --help\n```\n\n## Expected Debug Output\n\nWhen Pluck debug logging is enabled, you should see the following events:\n\n### 1. Strand Evaluation Start\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Pluck strand evaluation starting\n  exclude_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n  split_threshold=3\n```\n\n### 2. Bead Store Query\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Querying bead store for ready candidates\n  filters=Filters { \n    assignee: None, \n    exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] \n  }\n```\n\n### 3. Query Results\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Bead store returned N candidates\n  count=5\n```\n\n### 4. Label Filtering\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Filtering by excluded labels\n  excluded_beads=[\"bf-1234\", \"bf-5678\"]\n  reasons=[\"label:deferred\", \"label:blocked\"]\n```\n\n### 5. Status/Assignee Filtering\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Filtering by status and assignee\n  remaining=3\n```\n\n### 6. Sorting\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Sorting candidates by priority\n  first_candidate=\"bf-abcd\"\n```\n\n### 7. Split Decision\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Checking split threshold\n  failure_count=2\n  split_threshold=3\n  should_split=false\n```\n\n### 8. Final Result\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Strand evaluation complete\n  result=BeadFound(\"bf-abcd\")\n```\n\n## Manual Configuration\n\nIf you prefer to set up the environment manually:\n\n```bash\n# Set the desired debug level\nexport RUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n\n# Run NEEDLE\ncd /home/coding/NEEDLE\ncargo run -- run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n\n# Or with output capture\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n## Log Analysis\n\nAfter capturing logs, analyze them with these commands:\n\n```bash\n# View all Pluck-related events\ngrep -i \"pluck\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Filter specific decisions\ngrep -i \"filter\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Check excluded beads\ngrep -i \"exclude\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# View candidate processing\ngrep -i \"candidate\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Check split decisions\ngrep -i \"split\" pluck-debug.log\n\n# Count events by type\ngrep -c \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=BeadFound\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=NoWork\" pluck-debug.log\ngrep -c \"result=Split\" pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### No Pluck output visible\n\n1. **Check RUST_LOG is set correctly:**\n   ```bash\n   echo $RUST_LOG\n   ```\n\n2. **Verify Pluck strand is active:**\n   ```bash\n   grep \"worker booted\" pluck-debug.log | grep \"pluck\"\n   ```\n\n3. **Ensure beads are available for processing:**\n   ```bash\n   br list --status=open\n   ```\n\n### Bead store query failed\n\nThis error indicates the Pluck strand cannot access the bead store:\n```\nERROR needle::strand::pluck: Bead store query failed error=bf list failed\n```\n\n**Possible causes:**\n- Bead store locked by another process\n- Corrupted bead database\n- Permission issues\n\n**Resolution:**\n```bash\n# Check bead store integrity\ncd /home/coding/ARMOR\nbr doctor --repair\n```\n\n## Configuration Files\n\n### Script Configuration\nThe main configuration is in the script itself:\n- **Location:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/pluck-debug-config.sh`\n- **Configuration:** Lines 20-27 contain the preset definitions\n\n### Environment Variables\n- **RUST_LOG:** Controls Rust crate-level logging\n- **RUST_BACKTRACE:** Set to `1` for backtraces on errors\n\n## Integration with NEEDLE\n\nThe Pluck strand is part of the standard NEEDLE strand set:\n```\nstrands=[\"pluck\", \"mend\", \"explore\", \"weave\", \"unravel\", \"pulse\", \"reflect\", \"splice\", \"knot\"]\n```\n\nPluck is the first strand evaluated and is responsible for:\n1. Querying the bead store for ready beads\n2. Filtering by labels (deferred, human, blocked)\n3. Filtering by assignee\n4. Sorting candidates by priority\n5. Checking for split conditions\n6. Returning NoWork/BeadFound/Split result\n\n## Next Steps\n\nOnce debug logging is configured:\n\n1. **Run a test capture:**\n   ```bash\n   ./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR test-output.log standard\n   ```\n\n2. **Analyze the output:**\n   ```bash\n   grep \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" test-output.log\n   ```\n\n3. **Filter for specific decisions:**\n   ```bash\n   grep -A 5 \"Filtering by\" test-output.log\n   ```\n\n4. **Verify expected behavior:**\n   - Check that excluded labels are properly filtered\n   - Verify split threshold logic\n   - Confirm candidate selection order\n\n## Status\n\n✅ **Debug logging infrastructure:** Configured and operational  \n✅ **Configuration script:** Created and executable  \n✅ **Preset configurations:** 6 levels available  \n✅ **Documentation:** Complete  \n✅ **Ready for execution:** Yes  \n\nThe Pluck debug logging configuration is complete and ready for use.","numLines":275,"startLine":1,"totalLines":275}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_58d0f3c9bb1844c9a956a882","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# Pluck Debug Flags Reference\n2\t\n3\t**Bead:** bf-4ejd\n4\t**Date:** 2026-07-09\n5\t**Task:** Identify Pluck debug flags and command structure\n6\t\n7\t## Overview\n8\t\n9\tPluck (part of the NEEDLE system) uses the Rust `RUST_LOG` environment variable for debug logging. There are no CLI debug flags - all debug control is via environment variables.\n10\t\n11\t## Primary Debug Flags\n12\t\n13\t### RUST_LOG (Primary Debug Control)\n14\t\n15\t**Purpose:** Controls Rust crate-level logging verbosity\n16\t\n17\t**Format:** `RUST_LOG=<module_path>=<level>`\n18\t\n19\t**Available Levels:**\n20\t- `error` - Only errors\n21\t- `warn` - Warnings and errors\n22\t- `info` - High-level operations\n23\t- `debug` - Detailed debugging information\n24\t- `trace` - Complete execution trace (most verbose)\n25\t\n26\t### RUST_BACKTRACE (Error Debugging)\n27\t\n28\t**Purpose:** Enables stack traces on errors\n29\t\n30\t**Values:**\n31\t- `0` or unset - No backtraces (default)\n32\t- `1` - Full backtraces on errors\n33\t\n34\t## Pluck-Specific Module Paths\n35\t\n36\t### Core Pluck Module\n37\t```\n38\tneedle::strand::pluck\n39\t```\n40\t\n41\t### Supporting Modules\n42\t```\n43\tneedle::strand          - General strand operations\n44\tneedle::bead_store      - Bead database operations\n45\tneedle::worker          - Worker lifecycle\n46\tneedle::dispatch        - Bead dispatch logic\n47\tneedle::claim           - Bead claiming logic\n48\t```\n49\t\n50\t## Recommended Configurations\n51\t\n52\t### 1. Standard Debugging (Recommended)\n53\t```bash\n54\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n55\t```\n56\t**Output:** Filtering decisions, statistics, candidate processing\n57\t\n58\t### 2. Detailed Trace\n59\t```bash\n60\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace\n61\t```\n62\t**Output:** Complete execution flow, all decision points\n63\t\n64\t### 3. Comprehensive (Multi-Module)\n65\t```bash\n66\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\n67\t```\n68\t**Output:** Pluck trace + supporting modules at debug level\n69\t\n70\t### 4. Full System Debug\n71\t```bash\n72\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug,needle::claim=debug\n73\t```\n74\t**Output:** All critical NEEDLE modules at DEBUG/TRACE\n75\t\n76\t### 5. Maximum Verbosity\n77\t```bash\n78\tRUST_LOG=trace\n79\t```\n80\t**Output:** Everything at TRACE level (very verbose, all Rust crates)\n81\t\n82\t### 6. Minimal Logging\n83\t```bash\n84\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=info\n85\t```\n86\t**Output:** High-level operations only\n87\t\n88\t## Command Structure\n89\t\n90\t### Basic Command Pattern\n91\t```bash\n92\tRUST_LOG=<level> needle run -w <workspace> -c <count>\n93\t```\n94\t\n95\t### With Output Capture\n96\t```bash\n97\tRUST_LOG=<level> needle run -w <workspace> -c <count> 2>&1 | tee <output.log>\n98\t```\n99\t\n100\t### With Multiple Modules\n101\t```bash\n102\tRUST_LOG=\"module1=level1,module2=level2\" needle run -w <workspace> -c <count>\n103\t```\n104\t\n105\t## needle run CLI Options\n106\t\n107\t### Required Options\n108\t- `-w, --workspace <WORKSPACE>` - Path to workspace directory\n109\t- `-c, --count <COUNT>` - Number of workers to launch [default: 1]\n110\t\n111\t### Optional Options\n112\t- `-a, --agent <AGENT>` - Agent adapter to use\n113\t- `-i, --identifier <IDENTIFIER>` - Worker identifier (overrides NATO naming)\n114\t- `-t, --timeout <TIMEOUT>` - Agent execution timeout in seconds\n115\t- `--resume` - Resume an existing worker session\n116\t- `--hot-reload <true|false>` - Enable hot-reload for this worker\n117\t\n118\t## Complete Examples\n119\t\n120\t### Example 1: Standard Debug (Recommended)\n121\t```bash\n122\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n123\t```\n124\t\n125\t### Example 2: Detailed Trace with Output Capture\n126\t```bash\n127\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-trace.log\n128\t```\n129\t\n130\t### Example 3: Comprehensive Multi-Module Debug\n131\t```bash\n132\tRUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\" needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-comprehensive.log\n133\t```\n134\t\n135\t### Example 4: With Backtrace on Errors\n136\t```bash\n137\tRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug RUST_BACKTRACE=1 needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n138\t```\n139\t\n140\t### Example 5: Full System Debug\n141\t```bash\n142\tRUST_LOG=\"trace\" needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee full-trace.log\n143\t```\n144\t\n145\t## Automated Configuration Script\n146\t\n147\tA helper script is available at `/home/coding/ARMOR/pluck-debug-config.sh` with preset configurations:\n148\t\n149\t```bash\n150\t# Standard debug (recommended)\n151\t./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log standard\n152\t\n153\t# Detailed trace\n154\t./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log detailed\n155\t\n156\t# Comprehensive multi-module\n157\t./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log comprehensive\n158\t\n159\t# Full system debug\n160\t./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log full\n161\t```\n162\t\n163\tAvailable presets:\n164\t- `minimal` - INFO level\n165\t- `standard` - DEBUG level (recommended)\n166\t- `detailed` - TRACE level\n167\t- `comprehensive` - TRACE + supporting modules\n168\t- `full` - All NEEDLE modules DEBUG/TRACE\n169\t- `maximum` - Global TRACE\n170\t\n171\t## Expected Debug Output Events\n172\t\n173\tWhen Pluck debug logging is enabled at `debug` or `trace` level, you should see:\n174\t\n175\t1. **Strand evaluation start**\n176\t   - Exclude labels\n177\t   - Split threshold\n178\t\n179\t2. **Bead store query**\n180\t   - Filter parameters\n181\t   - Candidate count\n182\t\n183\t3. **Label filtering**\n184\t   - Excluded beads\n185\t   - Exclusion reasons\n186\t\n187\t4. **Status/assignee filtering**\n188\t   - Remaining candidates\n189\t\n190\t5. **Candidate sorting**\n191\t   - Priority order\n192\t\n193\t6. **Split decision**\n194\t   - Failure count vs threshold\n195\t   - Should split decision\n196\t\n197\t7. **Final result**\n198\t   - BeadFound / NoWork / Split\n199\t\n200\t## Log Analysis Commands\n201\t\n202\tAfter capturing logs, analyze them with:\n203\t\n204\t```bash\n205\t# All Pluck events\n206\tgrep -i \"pluck\" output.log\n207\t\n208\t# Filtering decisions\n209\tgrep -i \"filter\" output.log\n210\t\n211\t# Excluded beads\n212\tgrep -i \"exclude\" output.log\n213\t\n214\t# Candidate processing\n215\tgrep -i \"candidate\" output.log\n216\t\n217\t# Split decisions\n218\tgrep -i \"split\" output.log\n219\t\n220\t# Count event types\n221\tgrep -c \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" output.log\n222\tgrep -c \"result=BeadFound\" output.log\n223\tgrep -c \"result=NoWork\" output.log\n224\tgrep -c \"result=Split\" output.log\n225\t```\n226\t\n227\t## Key Findings Summary\n228\t\n229\t✅ **No CLI debug flags** - All debug control via environment variables\n230\t✅ **Primary flag:** `RUST_LOG` - Controls module-level logging\n231\t✅ **Secondary flag:** `RUST_BACKTRACE` - Enables error stack traces\n232\t✅ **Command structure:** `RUST_LOG=<level> needle run -w <workspace> -c <count>`\n233\t✅ **6 preset configurations** available via helper script\n234\t✅ **Ready to construct execution commands**\n235\t\n236\t## Status\n237\t\n238\t**COMPLETE** - There are no CLI debug flags - all debug control is via environment variables.\n\n## Primary Debug Flags\n\n### RUST_LOG (Primary Debug Control)\n\n**Purpose:** Controls Rust crate-level logging verbosity\n\n**Format:** `RUST_LOG=<module_path>=<level>`\n\n**Available Levels:**\n- `error` - Only errors\n- `warn` - Warnings and errors\n- `info` - High-level operations\n- `debug` - Detailed debugging information\n- `trace` - Complete execution trace (most verbose)\n\n### RUST_BACKTRACE (Error Debugging)\n\n**Purpose:** Enables stack traces on errors\n\n**Values:**\n- `0` or unset - No backtraces (default)\n- `1` - Full backtraces on errors\n\n## Pluck-Specific Module Paths\n\n### Core Pluck Module\n```\nneedle::strand::pluck\n```\n\n### Supporting Modules\n```\nneedle::strand          - General strand operations\nneedle::bead_store      - Bead database operations\nneedle::worker          - Worker lifecycle\nneedle::dispatch        - Bead dispatch logic\nneedle::claim           - Bead claiming logic\n```\n\n## Recommended Configurations\n\n### 1. Standard Debugging (Recommended)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug\n```\n**Output:** Filtering decisions, statistics, candidate processing\n\n### 2. Detailed Trace\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace\n```\n**Output:** Complete execution flow, all decision points\n\n### 3. Comprehensive (Multi-Module)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\n```\n**Output:** Pluck trace + supporting modules at debug level\n\n### 4. Full System Debug\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug,needle::claim=debug\n```\n**Output:** All critical NEEDLE modules at DEBUG/TRACE\n\n### 5. Maximum Verbosity\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=trace\n```\n**Output:** Everything at TRACE level (very verbose, all Rust crates)\n\n### 6. Minimal Logging\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=info\n```\n**Output:** High-level operations only\n\n## Command Structure\n\n### Basic Command Pattern\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=<level> needle run -w <workspace> -c <count>\n```\n\n### With Output Capture\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=<level> needle run -w <workspace> -c <count> 2>&1 | tee <output.log>\n```\n\n### With Multiple Modules\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=\"module1=level1,module2=level2\" needle run -w <workspace> -c <count>\n```\n\n## needle run CLI Options\n\n### Required Options\n- `-w, --workspace <WORKSPACE>` - Path to workspace directory\n- `-c, --count <COUNT>` - Number of workers to launch [default: 1]\n\n### Optional Options\n- `-a, --agent <AGENT>` - Agent adapter to use\n- `-i, --identifier <IDENTIFIER>` - Worker identifier (overrides NATO naming)\n- `-t, --timeout <TIMEOUT>` - Agent execution timeout in seconds\n- `--resume` - Resume an existing worker session\n- `--hot-reload <true|false>` - Enable hot-reload for this worker\n\n## Complete Examples\n\n### Example 1: Standard Debug (Recommended)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n### Example 2: Detailed Trace with Output Capture\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-trace.log\n```\n\n### Example 3: Comprehensive Multi-Module Debug\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\" needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-comprehensive.log\n```\n\n### Example 4: With Backtrace on Errors\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug RUST_BACKTRACE=1 needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n### Example 5: Full System Debug\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=\"trace\" needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee full-trace.log\n```\n\n## Automated Configuration Script\n\nA helper script is available at `/home/coding/ARMOR/pluck-debug-config.sh` with preset configurations:\n\n```bash\n# Standard debug (recommended)\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log standard\n\n# Detailed trace\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log detailed\n\n# Comprehensive multi-module\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log comprehensive\n\n# Full system debug\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log full\n```\n\nAvailable presets:\n- `minimal` - INFO level\n- `standard` - DEBUG level (recommended)\n- `detailed` - TRACE level\n- `comprehensive` - TRACE + supporting modules\n- `full` - All NEEDLE modules DEBUG/TRACE\n- `maximum` - Global TRACE\n\n## Expected Debug Output Events\n\nWhen Pluck debug logging is enabled at `debug` or `trace` level, you should see:\n\n1. **Strand evaluation start**\n   - Exclude labels\n   - Split threshold\n\n2. **Bead store query**\n   - Filter parameters\n   - Candidate count\n\n3. **Label filtering**\n   - Excluded beads\n   - Exclusion reasons\n\n4. **Status/assignee filtering**\n   - Remaining candidates\n\n5. **Candidate sorting**\n   - Priority order\n\n6. **Split decision**\n   - Failure count vs threshold\n   - Should split decision\n\n7. **Final result**\n   - BeadFound / NoWork / Split\n\n## Log Analysis Commands\n\nAfter capturing logs, analyze them with:\n\n```bash\n# All Pluck events\ngrep -i \"pluck\" output.log\n\n# Filtering decisions\ngrep -i \"filter\" output.log\n\n# Excluded beads\ngrep -i \"exclude\" output.log\n\n# Candidate processing\ngrep -i \"candidate\" output.log\n\n# Split decisions\ngrep -i \"split\" output.log\n\n# Count event types\ngrep -c \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" output.log\ngrep -c \"result=BeadFound\" output.log\ngrep -c \"result=NoWork\" output.log\ngrep -c \"result=Split\" output.log\n```\n\n## Key Findings Summary\n\n✅ **No CLI debug flags** - The complete command structure combines `RUST_LOG` environment variable configuration with standard `needle run` CLI options.\n\n## Complete Command Template\n\n### Basic Command Structure\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=<debug_level> needle run -w <workspace> -c <count>\n```\n\n### Full Command with All Options\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=<debug_level> needle run -w <workspace> -c <count> -a <agent> -i <identifier> -t <timeout> [--resume] [--hot-reload <true|false>]\n```\n\n## Primary Debug Environment Variable: RUST_LOG\n\n### Format\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=<module_path>=<level>[,<module_path>=<level>]\n```\n\n### Available Log Levels\n- **error** - Critical errors only\n- **warn** - Warnings and errors  \n- **info** - High-level operations\n- **debug** - Detailed debugging information (recommended)\n- **trace** - Complete execution trace (most verbose)\n\n### Pluck-Specific Module Paths\n\n#### Core Module\n```\nneedle::strand::pluck        # Primary Pluck strand logic\n```\n\n#### Supporting Modules\n```\nneedle::strand              # General strand operations\nneedle::bead_store          # Bead database operations  \nneedle::worker              # Worker lifecycle management\nneedle::dispatch            # Bead dispatch logic\nneedle::claim               # Bead claiming logic\n```\n\n## Six Complete Command Configurations\n\n### 1. Minimal Logging (INFO level)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=info needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n**Purpose:** Quick health checks, basic operation verification  \n**Output:** High-level strand operations only\n\n### 2. Standard Debug (Recommended)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n**Purpose:** Normal debugging, understanding filtering behavior  \n**Output:** Filtering decisions and statistics\n\n### 3. Detailed Trace\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n**Purpose:** Deep troubleshooting, understanding exact flow  \n**Output:** Complete execution details\n\n### 4. Comprehensive Multi-Module\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\" needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n**Purpose:** Full context debugging, understanding system interactions  \n**Output:** Pluck TRACE + supporting modules DEBUG\n\n### 5. Full System Debug\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug,needle::dispatch=debug,needle::claim=debug\" needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n**Purpose:** Complete system debugging  \n**Output:** All critical NEEDLE modules at DEBUG/TRACE\n\n### 6. Maximum Verbosity (Global TRACE)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=trace needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n**Purpose:** Deep system-level debugging  \n**Output:** Everything at TRACE level (very verbose)\n\n## Commands with Output Capture\n\n### Standard Debug with Log File\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n### Detailed Trace with Timestamped Log\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log\n```\n\n### Comprehensive Multi-Module with Backtrace Support\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\" RUST_BACKTRACE=1 needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-comprehensive.log\n```\n\n## needle run CLI Options\n\n### Required Options\n```bash\n-w, --workspace <WORKSPACE>    # Path to workspace directory\n-c, --count <COUNT>            # Number of workers to launch [default: 1]\n```\n\n### Optional Options\n```bash\n-a, --agent <AGENT>            # Agent adapter to use\n-i, --identifier <IDENTIFIER>  # Worker identifier (overrides NATO naming)\n-t, --timeout <TIMEOUT>        # Agent execution timeout in seconds\n--resume                       # Resume an existing worker session\n--hot-reload <true|false>      # Enable hot-reload for this worker\n```\n\n## Automated Configuration Script\n\n### Script Location\n```bash\n/home/coding/ARMOR/pluck-debug-config.sh\n```\n\n### Usage Examples\n```bash\n# Standard debug (recommended)\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log standard\n\n# Detailed trace\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log detailed\n\n# Comprehensive multi-module\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log comprehensive\n\n# Full system debug with 3 workers\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log full 3\n```\n\n### Available Presets\n- `minimal` - INFO level\n- `standard` - DEBUG level (recommended)\n- `detailed` - TRACE level\n- `comprehensive` - TRACE + supporting modules\n- `full` - All NEEDLE modules DEBUG/TRACE\n- `maximum` - Global TRACE\n\n## Secondary Debug Environment Variables\n\n### RUST_BACKTRACE\n```bash\nRUST_BACKTRACE=1    # Enable full backtraces on errors\nRUST_BACKTRACE=0    # Disable backtraces (default)\n```\n\n**Usage Example:**\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug RUST_BACKTRACE=1 needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n## Expected Debug Output Events\n\nWhen Pluck debug logging is enabled at `debug` or `trace` level, you should see:\n\n### 1. Strand Evaluation Start\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Pluck strand evaluation starting\n  exclude_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n  split_threshold=3\n```\n\n### 2. Bead Store Query\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Querying bead store for ready candidates\n  filters=Filters { \n    assignee: None, \n    exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] \n  }\n```\n\n### 3. Query Results\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Bead store returned N candidates\n  count=5\n```\n\n### 4. Label Filtering\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Filtering by excluded labels\n  excluded_beads=[\"bf-1234\", \"bf-5678\"]\n  reasons=[\"label:deferred\", \"label:blocked\"]\n```\n\n### 5. Status/Assignee Filtering\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Filtering by status and assignee\n  remaining=3\n```\n\n### 6. Sorting\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Sorting candidates by priority\n  first_candidate=\"bf-abcd\"\n```\n\n### 7. Split Decision\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Checking split threshold\n  failure_count=2\n  split_threshold=3\n  should_split=false\n```\n\n### 8. Final Result\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Strand evaluation complete\n  result=BeadFound(\"bf-abcd\")\n```\n\n## Log Analysis Commands\n\n### Filter Specific Events\n```bash\n# All Pluck events\ngrep -i \"pluck\" output.log\n\n# Filtering decisions\ngrep -i \"filter\" output.log\n\n# Excluded beads\ngrep -i \"exclude\" output.log\n\n# Candidate processing\ngrep -i \"candidate\" output.log\n\n# Split decisions\ngrep -i \"split\" output.log\n\n# Strand evaluation lifecycle\ngrep -i \"evaluation\" output.log\n```\n\n### Count Event Types\n```bash\ngrep -c \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" output.log\ngrep -c \"result=BeadFound\" output.log\ngrep -c \"result=NoWork\" output.log\ngrep -c \"result=Split\" output.log\n```\n\n### Multi-line Context\n```bash\n# Show 5 lines after each filter event\ngrep -A 5 \"Filtering by\" output.log\n\n# Show context around split decisions\ngrep -B 3 -A 3 \"split threshold\" output.log\n```\n\n## Quick Reference Table\n\n| Debug Level | RUST_LOG Value | Verbosity | Use Case |\n|------------|----------------|-----------|----------|\n| Minimal | `needle::strand::pluck=info` | Low | Health checks |\n| Standard | `needle::strand::pluck=debug` | Medium | Normal debugging (recommended) |\n| Detailed | `needle::strand::pluck=trace` | High | Deep troubleshooting |\n| Comprehensive | `needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug` | High | System context |\n| Full | All NEEDLE modules at DEBUG/TRACE | Very High | Complete system debug |\n| Maximum | `trace` | Extreme | Deep system analysis |\n\n## Complete Example Session\n\n### Session Setup\n```bash\n# 1. Navigate to workspace\ncd /home/coding/ARMOR\n\n# 2. Create log directory\nmkdir -p logs/pluck-debug\n\n# 3. Run with standard debug level\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee logs/pluck-debug/pluck_debug_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log\n\n# 4. Analyze the output\ngrep -i \"pluck\" logs/pluck-debug/pluck_debug_*.log\ngrep -A 5 \"Filtering by\" logs/pluck-debug/pluck_debug_*.log\n```\n\n### Using the Automated Script\n```bash\n# Quick start with recommended settings\ncd /home/coding/ARMOR\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR logs/pluck-debug/output.log standard\n\n# Comprehensive debugging\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR logs/pluck-debug/comprehensive.log comprehensive\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### No Pluck Output Visible\n1. **Check RUST_LOG is set correctly:**\n   ```bash\n   echo $RUST_LOG\n   ```\n\n2. **Verify Pluck strand is active:**\n   ```bash\n   grep \"worker booted\" output.log | grep \"pluck\"\n   ```\n\n3. **Ensure beads are available for processing:**\n   ```bash\n   br list --status=open\n   ```\n\n### Bead Store Query Failed\n**Error:** `ERROR needle::strand::pluck: Bead store query failed`\n\n**Possible causes:**\n- Bead store locked by another process\n- Corrupted bead database  \n- Permission issues\n\n**Resolution:**\n```bash\ncd /home/coding/ARMOR\nbr doctor --repair\n```\n\n## Acceptance Criteria Verification\n\n✅ **Complete Pluck command documented with all debug flags**\n- All 6 debug configurations documented\n- Environment variables explained\n- CLI options detailed\n\n✅ **Command string saved to reference file**\n- This document: `/home/coding/ARMOR/notes/bf-7423-pluck-debug-command-reference.md`\n\n✅ **Each debug flag's purpose documented**\n- RUST_LOG levels explained\n- Module paths documented\n- Expected output events described\n\n## Status\n\n**COMPLETE** - Maximum Verbosity (Global TRACE)\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=trace needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n**Purpose:** Deep system-level debugging  \n**Output:** Everything at TRACE level (very verbose)\n\n## Commands with Output Capture\n\n### Standard Debug with Log File\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug.log\n```\n\n### Detailed Trace with Timestamped Log\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=trace needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-debug-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log\n```\n\n### Comprehensive Multi-Module with Backtrace Support\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=\"needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug\" RUST_BACKTRACE=1 needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1 2>&1 | tee pluck-comprehensive.log\n```\n\n## needle run CLI Options\n\n### Required Options\n```bash\n-w, --workspace <WORKSPACE>    # Path to workspace directory\n-c, --count <COUNT>            # Number of workers to launch [default: 1]\n```\n\n### Optional Options\n```bash\n-a, --agent <AGENT>            # Agent adapter to use\n-i, --identifier <IDENTIFIER>  # Worker identifier (overrides NATO naming)\n-t, --timeout <TIMEOUT>        # Agent execution timeout in seconds\n--resume                       # Resume an existing worker session\n--hot-reload <true|false>      # Enable hot-reload for this worker\n```\n\n## Automated Configuration Script\n\n### Script Location\n```bash\n/home/coding/ARMOR/pluck-debug-config.sh\n```\n\n### Usage Examples\n```bash\n# Standard debug (recommended)\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log standard\n\n# Detailed trace\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log detailed\n\n# Comprehensive multi-module\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log comprehensive\n\n# Full system debug with 3 workers\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR output.log full 3\n```\n\n### Available Presets\n- `minimal` - INFO level\n- `standard` - DEBUG level (recommended)\n- `detailed` - TRACE level\n- `comprehensive` - TRACE + supporting modules\n- `full` - All NEEDLE modules DEBUG/TRACE\n- `maximum` - Global TRACE\n\n## Secondary Debug Environment Variables\n\n### RUST_BACKTRACE\n```bash\nRUST_BACKTRACE=1    # Enable full backtraces on errors\nRUST_BACKTRACE=0    # Disable backtraces (default)\n```\n\n**Usage Example:**\n```bash\nRUST_LOG=needle::strand::pluck=debug RUST_BACKTRACE=1 needle run -w /home/coding/ARMOR -c 1\n```\n\n## Expected Debug Output Events\n\nWhen Pluck debug logging is enabled at `debug` or `trace` level, you should see:\n\n### 1. Strand Evaluation Start\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Pluck strand evaluation starting\n  exclude_labels=[\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"]\n  split_threshold=3\n```\n\n### 2. Bead Store Query\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Querying bead store for ready candidates\n  filters=Filters { \n    assignee: None, \n    exclude_labels: [\"deferred\", \"human\", \"blocked\"] \n  }\n```\n\n### 3. Query Results\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Bead store returned N candidates\n  count=5\n```\n\n### 4. Label Filtering\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Filtering by excluded labels\n  excluded_beads=[\"bf-1234\", \"bf-5678\"]\n  reasons=[\"label:deferred\", \"label:blocked\"]\n```\n\n### 5. Status/Assignee Filtering\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Filtering by status and assignee\n  remaining=3\n```\n\n### 6. Sorting\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Sorting candidates by priority\n  first_candidate=\"bf-abcd\"\n```\n\n### 7. Split Decision\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Checking split threshold\n  failure_count=2\n  split_threshold=3\n  should_split=false\n```\n\n### 8. Final Result\n```\nDEBUG needle::strand::pluck: Strand evaluation complete\n  result=BeadFound(\"bf-abcd\")\n```\n\n## Log Analysis Commands\n\n### Filter Specific Events\n```bash\n# All Pluck events\ngrep -i \"pluck\" output.log\n\n# Filtering decisions\ngrep -i \"filter\" output.log\n\n# Excluded beads\ngrep -i \"exclude\" output.log\n\n# Candidate processing\ngrep -i \"candidate\" output.log\n\n# Split decisions\ngrep -i \"split\" output.log\n\n# Strand evaluation lifecycle\ngrep -i \"evaluation\" output.log\n```\n\n### Count Event Types\n```bash\ngrep -c \"Pluck strand evaluation starting\" output.log\ngrep -c \"result=BeadFound\" output.log\ngrep -c \"result=NoWork\" output.log\ngrep -c \"result=Split\" output.log\n```\n\n### Multi-line Context\n```bash\n# Show 5 lines after each filter event\ngrep -A 5 \"Filtering by\" output.log\n\n# Show context around split decisions\ngrep -B 3 -A 3 \"split threshold\" output.log\n```\n\n## Quick Reference Table\n\n| Debug Level | RUST_LOG Value | Verbosity | Use Case |\n|------------|----------------|-----------|----------|\n| Minimal | `needle::strand::pluck=info` | Low | Health checks |\n| Standard | `needle::strand::pluck=debug` | Medium | Normal debugging (recommended) |\n| Detailed | `needle::strand::pluck=trace` | High | Deep troubleshooting |\n| Comprehensive | `needle::strand::pluck=trace,needle::strand=debug,needle::bead_store=debug,needle::worker=debug` | High | System context |\n| Full | All NEEDLE modules at DEBUG/TRACE | Very High | Complete system debug |\n| Maximum | `trace` | Extreme | Deep system analysis |\n\n## Complete Example Session\n\n### Session Setup\n```bash\n# 1. Analyze the output\ngrep -i \"pluck\" logs/pluck-debug/pluck_debug_*.log\ngrep -A 5 \"Filtering by\" logs/pluck-debug/pluck_debug_*.log\n```\n\n### Using the Automated Script\n```bash\n# Quick start with recommended settings\ncd /home/coding/ARMOR\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR logs/pluck-debug/output.log standard\n\n# Comprehensive debugging\n./pluck-debug-config.sh /home/coding/ARMOR logs/pluck-debug/comprehensive.log comprehensive\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### No Pluck Output Visible\n1. **Check RUST_LOG is set correctly:**\n   ```bash\n   echo $RUST_LOG\n   ```\n\n2. **Verify Pluck strand is active:**\n   ```bash\n   grep \"worker booted\" output.log | grep \"pluck\"\n   ```\n\n3. **Ensure beads are available for processing:**\n   ```bash\n   br list --status=open\n   ```\n\n### Bead Store Query Failed\n**Error:** `ERROR needle::strand::pluck: Bead store query failed`\n\n**Possible causes:**\n- Bead store locked by another process\n- Corrupted bead database  \n- Permission issues\n\n**Resolution:**\n```bash\ncd /home/coding/ARMOR\nbr doctor --repair\n```\n\n## Acceptance Criteria Verification\n\n✅ **Complete Pluck command documented with all debug flags**\n- All 6 debug configurations documented\n- Environment variables explained\n- CLI options detailed\n\n✅ **Command string saved to reference file**\n- This document: `/home/coding/ARMOR/notes/bf-7423-pluck-debug-command-reference.md`\n\n✅ **Each debug flag's purpose documented**\n- RUST_LOG levels explained\n- Module paths documented\n- Expected output events described\n\n## Status\n\n**COMPLETE** - All Pluck debug commands constructed and documented\n- 6 complete command configurations provided\n- Automated script usage documented\n- Expected output and analysis commands included\n- Ready for immediate use\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null,"userModified":false}}
